etc/seed.purpose: Demo data seeding layer for OpenMeter. Contains Benthos/Redpanda Connect pipeline configurations that generate synthetic CloudEvents and POST them to the ingest API, plus a child `streams/` folder with domain-specific event generators (api-requests, prompt-tokens, workload-runtime).

patterns.name: Environment-variable parameterisation with defaults
patterns.description: Every runtime value (URL, token, count, interval, subject count) uses ${VAR:default} syntax so the file works out-of-the-box without env vars but is overridable in CI or production.

patterns.name: CloudEvents 1.0 envelope
patterns.description: Every generated event must include specversion, id (uuid_v4()), type, source, subject, time at root. Dimensional/domain attributes belong inside data: {}, not at the envelope root.

patterns.name: Infinite generation with count: 0
patterns.description: All seed streams use count: ${SEEDER_COUNT:0} so they run indefinitely by default. count > 0 terminates the stream and breaks the seeder's purpose.

patterns.name: Subject pool via modulo randomisation with timestamp seed
patterns.description: Subjects are drawn from a bounded pool (customer-0..N) using random_int(seed: timestamp_unix_nano()) % $max_subjects. Always seed from timestamp_unix_nano() — a fixed seed produces a non-random sequence.

patterns.name: Dual-output switch: HTTP primary + optional stdout log
patterns.description: output.switch always has at least two cases: an unconditional HTTP POST to the ingest API and a conditional stdout logger gated by SEEDER_LOG=true.
patterns.example:
output:
  switch:
    cases:
      - check: ""
        continue: true
        output:
          http_client: ...
      - check: '"${SEEDER_LOG:false}" == "true"'
        output:
          stdout: ...

key_file_guides.role: Top-level seed stream for token usage events. Generates 'tokens' CloudEvents with provider/model/type/count dimensions. Entry point for `benthos -c etc/seed/seed.yaml`.
key_file_guides.watch_for: count: ${SEEDER_COUNT:0} must stay 0-defaulted. The commented-out time backdating block shows how to generate historical data — keep it commented unless historical seeding is intentional.

key_file_guides.role: OTel tracing config for the Benthos seed process itself. Sends traces to a local collector on :4317 with sampling disabled by default.
key_file_guides.watch_for: sampling.enabled defaults to false — enabling it in dev adds no user value and can flood a local collector.

key_file_guides.role: Generates synthetic API request events (child stream).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must follow identical input.generate + output.switch structure as seed.yaml.

key_file_guides.role: Generates LLM prompt token events (child stream).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Token counts must be in data: {} not at envelope root.

key_file_guides.role: Generates compute workload runtime events (child stream).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Same structural contract as other streams — deviation breaks batch seeding.

anti_patterns: Hardcoding OPENMETER_TOKEN or OPENMETER_BASE_URL — always use ${VAR:default}
Setting count > 0 — seed streams must run indefinitely
Putting dimensional attributes outside data: {} (they belong in data, not at CloudEvent root)
Using a fixed random seed instead of timestamp_unix_nano() — produces a repeating non-random sequence
Adding a non-CloudEvents-1.0 envelope (omitting specversion, id, or time breaks the ingest API validation)

decisions.decision: All streams share the identical input.generate + output.switch skeleton; only the bloblang mapping body varies.
decisions.rationale: Consistency makes it trivial to add new event types without introducing structural drift that would break the Benthos pipeline runner.

decisions.decision: Subject space is bounded by SEEDER_SUBJECT_COUNT rather than purely random UUIDs.
decisions.rationale: Bounded subjects produce realistic per-customer usage curves and allow meter aggregations to produce non-trivial, non-sparse results in the demo UI.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new seed stream for a new event type
code_examples.code:
# etc/seed/streams/my-event.yaml
input:
  generate:
    count: ${SEEDER_COUNT:0}
    interval: "${SEEDER_INTERVAL:50ms}"
    mapping: |
      let max_subjects = ${SEEDER_MAX_SUBJECTS:20}
      let subject = "customer-%d".format(random_int(seed: timestamp_unix_nano()) % $max_subjects)
      root = {
        "id": uuid_v4(),
        "specversion": "1.0",
        "type": "my-event-type",
        "source": "my-source",
        "subject": $subject,
        "time": now().ts_format(),
        "data": {
          "my_dimension": "value"
        }
      }
output:
  switch:
    cases:
      - check: ""
        continue: true
        output:
          http_client:
            url: ${OPENMETER_BASE_URL:http://127.0.0.1:8888}/api/v1/events
            verb: POST
            headers:
              Content-Type: application/cloudevents+json
              Authorization: "Bearer ${OPENMETER_TOKEN:}"
            max_in_flight: 1
      - check: '"${SEEDER_LOG:false}" == "true"'
        output:
          stdout:
            codec: lines
